get.description:
This endpoint returns a list of companies related to the firm.

> ℹ️
> This endpoint requires the following scope: `companies:readonly`

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Items are paginated, this is the current page which will be returned. The page index is starting at 1.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Items are paginated. By default, you get 20 items per page. You can specify another number of items per page.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
You can choose to filter items on specific fields.
Available fields and values:
- `client_code`: `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`

200.description: Returns a list of companies related to the firm the access token is associated with.

city.description: The city the company is in.

postal_code.description: The postal/zip code of the company.

activity_nomenclature.description: The activity nomenclature code, e.g. "code NAF" in France.

external_id.description: The external identifier of the company.

total_pages.description: The total number of pages available

total_items.description: The total number of items available

per_page.description: The number of items per page

401.description: Access token is missing or invalid

error.example: Access to this resource requires scope "ledger".

post.summary: Export file of Analytical General Ledger
post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create an Analytical General Ledger export. The generated export file is an xlsx file, using the in-line analytical mode by default.

> ℹ️
> This endpoint requires the following scope: `exports:agl`

parameters.name: company_id
parameters.description: Existing company identifier (id)

period_start.description: Start date of the period to export

period_end.description: End date of the period to export

mode.description: The mode of the export. The export can be in lines or in columns. If this parameter is not provided, it will use the default `in_line` mode.

status.description:
State of the export
  - `pending` : The export is being generated
  - `ready` : The export is ready to be downloaded
  - `error` : An error occurred during the export generation

401.description: Access token is missing or invalid

error.example: Access to this resource requires scope "ledger".

get.description:
Returns a list of ledger entries.
By default, entries from fiscal periods that are closed or frozen are excluded.
However, if a 'date' filter is provided, it will return all entries within the specified date range, even if they fall within a closed or frozen fiscal period.


> ℹ️
> This endpoint requires one of the following scopes: `ledger_entries:all`, `ledger_entries:readonly`

parameters.name: company_id
parameters.description: Existing company identifier (id)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination. Use this to fetch the next set of results. The cursor is an opaque string returned in the previous response's metadata. Leave empty for the first request.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Number of items to return per request.
Defaults to 20 if not specified.
Must be between 1 and 1000.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
You can choose to filter items on specific fields.
Available fields and values:
- `id`,`journal_id`: `lt, lteq, gt, gteq, eq, not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`
- `date`: `lt, lteq, gt, gteq, eq, not_eq`

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
You can choose to sort items on specific attributes
Sort field may be prefixed with `-` for descending order.
Example : `date` will sort by ascending order, `-date` will sort by descending order.
Available fields : `id`, `date`

200.description:
Returns a list of ledger entries.
By default, entries from fiscal periods that are closed or frozen are excluded.
However, if a 'date' filter is provided, it will return all entries within the specified date range, even if they fall within a closed or frozen fiscal period.

created_at.description: Created at

updated_at.description: Last update

label.description: Label that describes the ledger entry

due_date.description: The date the invoice is due

invoice_number.description: Invoice number of the document linked to the ledger entry

status.description:
The accounting state of the entry.
- `archived`: The entry has been archived.
- `draft`: The customer invoice is still in draft and has not been finalized.
- `entry`: The invoice is incomplete, some information is missing and needs to be completed.
- `waiting_details`: The entry is awaiting additional accounting details.
- `validation_needed`: The entry is sent to the accountant and needs validation.
- `accounting_needed`: The entry needs to be processed by the accountant.
- `complete`: The entry has been validated by the accountant.
- `null` : The entry is neither a transaction nor an invoice that needs to be processed on Pennylane

attachment.description: The file attached to the ledger entry.

url.description: URL to the uploaded file.

has_more.description:
Indicates whether additional results are available beyond this set.
Use this flag to determine if another request is needed.

next_cursor.description:
Cursor to retrieve the next set of results.
Include this value in the cursor parameter of your next request to fetch subsequent items.
A `null` `next_cursor` in the response indicates no further results.

401.description: Access token is missing or invalid

get.description:
Returns the list of changes based on the provided `start_date`. If no `start_date` is provided it returns the oldest set of recorded changes. Changes for the last 4 weeks are retained. The items will be returned using `processed_at` in ASC order (oldest first).


> ℹ️
> This endpoint requires one of the following scopes: `dms_files:all`, `dms_files:readonly`

parameters.name: company_id
parameters.description: Existing company identifier (id)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination. Use this to fetch the next set of results. The cursor is an opaque string returned in the previous response's metadata. Leave empty for the first request.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Number of items to return per request.
Defaults to 20 if not specified.
Must be between 1 and 1000.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Filter the changes based on the event date. The date should follow RFC3339 format. If no date is provided, the oldest changes will be returned. Changes for the last 4 weeks are retained, thus providing a `start_date` older than that will result in a 422 response. Providing both `start_date` and `cursor` parameters will result in a 400 response.

200.description: A list of DMS files changes

id.description: Unique identifier of the dms file record

processed_at.description: Timestamp when the event arrived in the change log pipeline

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the record was updated in the database (can vary due to data restoration)

created_at.description: Timestamp when the record was initially created

has_more.description:
Indicates whether additional results are available beyond this set.
Use this flag to determine if another request is needed.

next_cursor.description:
Cursor to retrieve the next set of results.
Include this value in the cursor parameter of your next request to fetch subsequent items.
A `null` `next_cursor` in the response indicates no further results.

401.description: Access token is missing or invalid

error.example: Access to this resource requires scope "ledger".
